_note: SYNTHETIC offline fixture mirroring GET /api/feed + the detail's `articles` (full bodies the reader reads). Story 1 is the corpus resignation-scandal cluster (§5.5 wire-collapse + primary); story 2 is extraordinary + wire-only (§5.6); story 3 is French (on-device translation, #54).

stories.fact: Valoria's finance minister Daniel Reyes resigned amid a procurement scandal.

claims.text: Valoria's finance minister Daniel Reyes resigned on Tuesday amid a widening procurement scandal.
claims.speaker: the finance ministry

claims.text: Three state contracts were routed through a shell company controlled by Reyes's brother-in-law.
An independent review of recent procurement decisions will commence under an external auditor.

articles.title: Statement on the resignation of the Minister of Finance
articles.body:
The Minister of Finance, Daniel Reyes, has today tendered his resignation, effective immediately.

An independent review of recent procurement decisions will commence under an external auditor. The findings will be made public on completion.

The Ministry thanks Mr Reyes for his service and will ensure continuity of operations. The Deputy Minister will assume the Minister's duties pending the appointment of a successor.

articles.title: Reyes steps down days after our shell-company revelations
articles.body:
Daniel Reyes stepped down from Valoria's finance ministry on Tuesday, days after this paper revealed that three state contracts had been routed through a shell company controlled by his brother-in-law.

Reyes denied wrongdoing but said an independent inquiry was warranted. Documents reviewed by this paper show the contracts, worth a combined 40 million, bypassed the usual competitive tender process.

The shell company was incorporated weeks before the first award and lists an address shared with a family business, according to corporate filings.

articles.title: Valoria finance minister resigns amid procurement scandal
articles.body:
Valoria's finance minister, Daniel Reyes, resigned on Tuesday amid a widening procurement scandal, the finance ministry said.

Reyes had faced mounting pressure over state contracts awarded to a firm linked to his family. 'I am stepping down to allow a full and independent inquiry,' he said in a statement.

The ministry said the resignation took effect immediately and that a deputy would assume his duties pending a successor.

articles.title: Valoria finance minister resigns amid procurement scandal
articles.body:
Valoria's finance minister, Daniel Reyes, resigned on Tuesday amid a widening procurement scandal, the finance ministry said.

Reyes had faced mounting pressure over state contracts awarded to a firm linked to his family. He said he was stepping down to allow a full and independent inquiry.

articles.title: Reyes quits as Valoria finance minister
articles.body:
Valoria's finance minister Daniel Reyes resigned on Tuesday amid a widening procurement scandal, the finance ministry said, according to AFP.

Reyes had faced mounting pressure over state contracts awarded to a firm linked to his family. He did not admit wrongdoing.

stories.fact: A leaked memo shows Country X's central bank secretly sold its entire gold reserve.

claims.text: Country X's central bank secretly sold its entire gold reserve, according to a leaked memo.
claims.speaker: a leaked memo

claims.text: Analysts warn the currency could collapse within a week.
claims.speaker: analysts

articles.title: Leaked memo: Country X sold its entire gold reserve in secret
articles.body:
A leaked internal memo suggests that Country X's central bank quietly sold its entire gold reserve over the past quarter, according to a document seen by Global Wire.

The bank has not confirmed the memo's authenticity, and Global Wire was unable to independently verify the document. Analysts cautioned that the claim rests on a single, unverified source.

'If it proved true, the currency could come under severe pressure within a week,' one analyst said, while stressing that the memo had not been authenticated.

articles.title: Country X 'dumped its gold', leaked memo claims
articles.body:
Country X's central bank secretly offloaded its gold reserves, a leaked memo claims, in a report first carried by Global Wire.

Officials did not respond to requests for comment. No second source has corroborated the document.

articles.title: Shock gold-reserve claims sweep markets
articles.body:
Shock claims that Country X dumped its entire gold stockpile circulated on Thursday, based on a single leaked memo.

The central bank has not commented, and the document remains unverified.

stories.fact: Wildfires near Marseille forced thousands to evacuate.

claims.text: Les incendies près de Marseille ont forcé des milliers de personnes à évacuer mardi soir.
claims.evidence_span: des milliers de personnes à évacuer

claims.text: La préfecture a déclaré l'état d'urgence dans trois communes.
claims.speaker: la préfecture

articles.title: Incendies près de Marseille : des milliers d'évacués
articles.body:
Les incendies qui ont éclaté près de Marseille ont forcé des milliers de personnes à évacuer mardi soir, alors que des vents violents poussaient les flammes vers les zones résidentielles.

Les pompiers, appuyés par des moyens aériens, ont lutté toute la nuit pour contenir le feu. Aucune victime n'était à déplorer dans l'immédiat, selon les secours.

Plusieurs routes ont été coupées et la circulation des trains régionaux interrompue.

articles.title: Communiqué : état d'urgence déclaré dans trois communes
articles.body:
La préfecture des Bouches-du-Rhône a déclaré l'état d'urgence dans trois communes en raison des incendies en cours.

Les habitants des secteurs concernés sont appelés à évacuer sans délai et à suivre les consignes des autorités. Des centres d'accueil ont été ouverts dans les communes voisines.

Les services de secours restent pleinement mobilisés.
